acceptance_targets: [chapter_17_craft_01] PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 leaves deletion ethically unresolved on the page: at least one speaker names a specific protection or knowledge they are losing by destroying the coordinate, no line explicitly labels the act as courage or responsibility, and the text immediately after the delete action still carries one concrete downside rather than thematic closure. FAIL if the scene still reads as a fully argued proof that deletion is simply the right answer.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 leaves deletion ethically unresolved on the page: at least one speaker names a specific protection or knowledge they are losing by destroying the coordinate, no line explicitly labels the act as courage or responsibility, and the text immediately after the delete action still carries one concrete downside rather than thematic closure. FAIL if the scene still reads as a fully argued proof that deletion is simply the right answer.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:207 frames the GPS dispute in grant-and-permit terms; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221 argue the anti-disclosure case; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273 explicitly defines deletion as courage rather than cowardice; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:301 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:305 execute the deletions after the scene has already morally ratified them; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:349 further sand down the report into a tidy protection logic.
findings.problem: The coordinate-deletion climax over-justifies itself on the page. Instead of leaving the choice ethically compromised, the scene repeatedly explains why deletion is the responsible, courageous answer and casts every institutional alternative as predatory. That moral safety valve reduces the ugliness of what they are actually doing: withholding precise data, planning to sandbag a report, and choosing opacity at the point of highest consequence. In this book's risk profile, that is sanitization drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 so the GPS argument stays morally double-edged. Keep the deletion, but cut or roughen the thesis-like justifications, add a concrete protection or scientific use they are sacrificing by deleting the mark, and let at least one character articulate the risk that this choice may also fail the creek later. Keep the exchange anchored to the live scene rather than letting it resolve into a clean statement of principle.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's uncomfortable overlaps: desire entangled with access, wonder contaminated by biotech history, class need shaping erotic choices, and ecological harm rendered at creature scale. When a scene sits on the edge between restraint and retreat, keep the pressure visible through bodies, residue, changed behavior, or social cost. If an exact detail would make the material more embarrassing, more sensual, or more morally compromising, that is usually the detail to keep.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, ecological damage, class shame, or moral compromise are softened into tasteful fog at the moment they should bite, the scene has failed and must be rewritten at higher specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms should signal deliberate emphasis, institutional performance, or a character-specific formality shift.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compression and dilation aggressively: single-scene nocturnes, comic paperwork chapters, quiet survey chapters, domestic weather chapters, confrontational set pieces, and aftermath chapters should sit beside each other without repeating the same rhythm two chapters in a row.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, coastal, and exact without brochure prettiness; scientific and bureaucratic terms may appear when the characters would think or speak through them, but they should stay embodied by consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Mina Cordero, with free-indirect access tight enough to track bodily desire, shame, and observational obsession without collapsing into confessional summary
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple sentence music that can hold humidity, rot, and erotic charge, then cut hard into blunt compression at moments of humiliation, procedural friction, or ecological damage.
